intro: Myriopteris rawsonii, also known as Rawson's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Myriopteris rawsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
